vulnerability.title: Enable authselect
vulnerability.description: Configure user authentication setup to use the authselect tool. If authselect profile is selected, the rule will enable the profile.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then var_authselect_profile='' authselect current if test "$?" -ne 0; then if { rpm --quiet -q kernel rpm-ostree bootc && ! rpm --quiet -q openshift-kubelet && { [ -f "/run/.containerenv" ] || [ -f "/.containerenv" ]; }; }; then authselect select --force "$var_authselect_profile" else authselect select "$var_authselect_profile" fi if test "$?" -ne 0; then if rpm --quiet --verify pam; then authselect select --force "$var_authselect_profile" else echo "authselect is not used but files from the 'pam' package have been altered, so the authselect configuration won't be forced." >&2 fi fi fi else >&2 echo 'Remediation is not applicable, nothing was done' fi

triage_decision.reason: Enabling authselect follows standard STIG hardening patterns for authentication and is unlikely to cause lockout or system breakage.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential misconfiguration could lock out users if an incorrect profile is selected

execution_details.stdout:
Profile ID: custom/hardening
Enabled features:
- with-faillock
- without-nullok
- with-smartcard

llm_verdict.message:
We performed an audit:

```bash
authselect current
```

The command returned a current profile, so the rule “enable_authselect” is satisfied. No file changes were needed.

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: The plan will effectively enable and enforce authselect with a minimal profile, addressing the vulnerability. However, a few enhancements are recommended to ensure compatibility and security consistency.
concerns: Hardcoding the 'minimal' profile may cause failures on distributions where this profile does not exist, and may not meet all organizational policy requirements.
Forcing a profile change when another profile is already correctly configured could inadvertently disrupt authentication functionality.
suggested_improvements: Verify the existence of the chosen profile using 'authselect list' before applying it, and provide a fallback or error message if it is unavailable.
Allow the profile to be parameterized (e.g., via an environment variable) rather than hardcoded, so the correct policy profile can be applied.
Create a backup of current PAM configuration files prior to forcing a profile change to allow safe rollback.
If 'authselect current' shows the system is already using the desired profile, skip the selection step to avoid unnecessary changes.
After applying a forced profile, run 'authselect apply-changes' only if additional PAM edits were made.
